Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. MICHAEL BRACAMONTES, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, APLC (tel: 415-835-6777) for Plaintiff.
Atty. LUCIA LI, Esq. of the City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-4206) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Requests For Documents, Set Two, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel.

The Court inquires as to why the parties could not reach a stipulation. The Court discusses along with counsel requests 27 through 33. Upon conclusion, the Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules as follows:

The Court denies the motion as to requests 27 and 28 based on Plaintiff's representation that she would not seek intetional infliction of emotional distress damages based on the kids' reactions.
The Court grants the motion as to request 29, and the plaintiff is to produce all responsive documents.
The Court grants the motion as to requests 30 and 31, narrowing the production to documents pertaining to the incident only.
The Court grants the motion as to requests 32 and 33, narrowing the production to communications to and from the Plaintiff that reflects either the temporary or permanent license plate. Counsel Michael Bracamontes is to unredact any documents already produced accordingly.
Plaintiff is to produce the documents by January 27, 2025. The Court is not awarding any sanctions at this time.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Joan Columbini, CSR# 5435 (tel: 510-367-3043 / email: joan.columbini.csr@gmail.com)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PRINCESS ABROGAR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions

Plaintiff Princess Abrogar's Motion For Sanctions is DENIED. The record on this motion does not establish Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Lars Nelson filed and litigated their Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication for an improper purpose, such as to harass Plaintiff, meriting sanctions. (See CCP section 128.7(a)&(b).) Nor does the record establish Defendants acted in bad faith or filed a frivolous motion or a motion that was solely intended to cause unnecessary delay, such that sanctions must or should be imposed. (See CCP section 128.5(a)&(b).) The record on this motion does not support imposition of monetary sanctions on Plaintiff based on her "ongoing tactics in this matter."

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
